description: Replace 22 errors.ErrUnsupported stubs in internal/meta/tikv/store.go (lines 100-186) with prod-ready implementations. Surfaced by ralph/storage-correctness US-010 smoke — `make smoke` against TiKV-default lab fails at TAGGING leg (HTTP 500). Cassandra backend implements all 22; TiKV stub'd them at US-001 of ralph/tikv-meta-backend cycle as 'fill in subsequent stories' but the cycle wrapped at US-015 before the cold S3 surface returned for impl. Pre-launch product per [Pre-launch no deploys] — no backwards-compat shims, no migration backfills. 6 stories: US-001 keycodec helper + tags + object grants (5 methods) → US-002 retention + legal-hold + restore-status (3 methods) → US-003 bucket grants (3 methods) → US-004 access points 5 methods (Create/Get/GetByAlias/Delete/List, snapshot read for chained alias lookup) → US-005 SSE rewrap + raw manifest + replication status (6 methods) → US-006 smoke + perf budget (TiKV/Cassandra p99 ratio ≤ 2×) + ROADMAP close-flip × 1 + ROADMAP format fix + PRD removal. Strict CI gate: testcontainers PD+TiKV must pass before merge.

userStories.title: Shared key codec + object tags + object grants
userStories.description: As an S3 client, I want PUT/GET/DELETE for object tags and per-object ACL grants on a TiKV-backed deployment. This story also lands the shared internal/meta/tikv/keycodec.go helper that US-002..US-005 reuse so the FoundationDB byte-stuffing pattern stays consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: New file internal/meta/tikv/keycodec.go exporting: PackKey(prefix string, segments ...interface{}) []byte — accepts string / []byte / uuid.UUID segments; applies FoundationDB byte-stuffing (`0x00 → 0x00 0xFF`, terminator `0x00 0x00`) to variable-length string + []byte segments per CLAUDE.md TiKV gotcha. UUID segments encoded as raw 16 bytes (no stuffing — fixed length). MarshalBlob(v interface{}) ([]byte, error) wraps json.Marshal. UnmarshalBlob(data []byte, v interface{}) error wraps json.Unmarshal. Unit tests keycodec_test.go: empty segments, segments containing 0x00, UUID encoding, round-trip.

5 TiKV impls in internal/meta/tikv/store.go using keycodec.PackKey + MarshalBlob/UnmarshalBlob: SetObjectTags (line 120), GetObjectTags (124), DeleteObjectTags (128), SetObjectGrants (112), GetObjectGrants (116).

**Key shape**: `object_meta:<bucket-uuid-raw-16>:<key-fdbstuffed>:<version-id-or-null-sentinel>:<kind>` where kind ∈ {tags, grants}. Null-versionId routing matches existing TiKV object CRUD convention.
**Payload**: JSON-blob per Cassandra parity. Tags: `map[string]string`. Grants: `[]meta.Grant`.

**Write path**: pessimistic txn per CLAUDE.md 'Plain Put on a key with prior LWT history breaks read-after-write' gotcha. Begin(pessimistic) → LockKeys → Get → Set/Delete → Commit. Explicit `txn.Rollback()` on every non-error early return per CLAUDE.md gotcha 'Pessimistic txns with EARLY-RETURN paths must call txn.Rollback() explicitly'.
**Read path**: snapshot Get is sufficient — no pessimistic txn for read-only.

**Delete semantics**: DeleteObjectTags removes the kind=tags key only; other kinds untouched. SetObjectTags / SetObjectGrants with empty collection writes empty-map / empty-list JSON blob (do NOT delete the row — empty != absent per S3 semantics).
**Cassandra parity** (verify against internal/meta/cassandra/store.go impls): same return shapes, same error sentinels (meta.ErrObjectNotFound when version doesn't exist), same null-handling.

**Contract test additions** in internal/meta/storetest/contract.go for 5 methods, each case block: (a) happy-path round-trip; (b) overwrite (Set twice, second wins); (c) delete (where applicable); (d) read on absent key returns zero value (NOT ErrObjectNotFound for tags — AWS returns empty TagSet on un-tagged object); (e) version-specific isolation (Set on v1 doesn't affect v2); (f) null-versionId routing.
Cassandra integration test parity — new contract cases pass against existing Cassandra factory without code changes.
TiKV integration test (`store_integration_test.go`) passes the new cases against testcontainers PD+TiKV.
`go vet ./...` passes; `go test -race ./internal/meta/...` passes.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Object retention + legal-hold + restore-status
userStories.description: As an S3 client, I want PUT/GET for Object Lock retention, legal-hold, and restore-status on a TiKV-backed deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 TiKV impls in internal/meta/tikv/store.go reusing keycodec helper from US-001: SetObjectRetention (line 132), SetObjectLegalHold (line 136), SetObjectRestoreStatus (line 140).
**Key shape**: `object_meta:<bucket-uuid-raw-16>:<key-fdbstuffed>:<version-id>:<kind>` where kind ∈ {retention, legal_hold, restore_status}. Consistent with US-001.

**Payload**: JSON-blob per Cassandra parity: retention {mode, until} (mode ∈ GOVERNANCE/COMPLIANCE, until RFC3339), legal_hold {on bool}, restore_status {status string}. Verify exact field names by reading corresponding Cassandra impls.
**Write path**: pessimistic txn with explicit Rollback on non-error early returns.

**COMPLIANCE retention reduction**: PRD ralph/storage-correctness US-006 added `objectlock:ComplianceRetentionAttemptedReduce` audit verb. This story's SetObjectRetention impl MUST preserve that wiring — when caller attempts to shorten a COMPLIANCE retention, return meta.ErrComplianceImmutable (verify sentinel exists; if not, add) and s3api layer stamps the audit row.
**Cassandra parity** check against internal/meta/cassandra/store.go corresponding impls — same return shape, same error sentinels.
**Contract test additions** in contract.go: for each of 3 methods, happy-path round-trip + version-specific isolation + COMPLIANCE reduction returns the sentinel.
`go vet ./...` passes; `go test -race ./internal/meta/...` passes for memory + Cassandra + TiKV.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Bucket grants — Set / Get / Delete
userStories.description: As an S3 client, I want PUT/GET/DELETE for bucket ACL grants on a TiKV-backed deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 TiKV impls replacing stubs at lines 100-109: SetBucketGrants, GetBucketGrants, DeleteBucketGrants.
**Key shape**: `bucket_meta:<bucket-uuid-raw-16>:grants`. Single PK per bucket (no version dimension).
**Payload**: JSON-blob of []meta.Grant via keycodec.MarshalBlob.
**Write path**: pessimistic txn with explicit Rollback on non-error early returns.
**Delete semantics**: DeleteBucketGrants removes the key; GetBucketGrants on absent key returns (nil, nil) NOT error — matches Cassandra impl; verify by reading the Cassandra method.
**Cassandra parity** check against internal/meta/cassandra/store.go:281-300 (SetBucketGrants/GetBucketGrants/DeleteBucketGrants) — same return shapes, same nil-handling.
**Contract test additions** in contract.go: happy-path round-trip; overwrite; delete; read on absent key returns (nil, nil).
`go vet ./...` passes; `go test -race ./internal/meta/...` passes across all three backends.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Access points — Create / Get / GetByAlias / Delete / List
userStories.description: As an operator using S3 Access Points on a TiKV-backed deployment, I want full lifecycle (create / get-by-name / get-by-alias / delete / list-by-bucket) so the access-point routing in internal/s3api/access_points.go works identically to Cassandra backend.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 TiKV impls replacing stubs at lines 144-161: CreateAccessPoint, GetAccessPoint, GetAccessPointByAlias, DeleteAccessPoint, ListAccessPoints.

**Three-index key shape** for coherent lookups: (a) `access_point:by_name:<name-fdbstuffed>` → full *meta.AccessPoint JSON blob; (b) `access_point:by_alias:<alias-fdbstuffed>` → name pointer; (c) `access_point:by_bucket:<bucket-uuid-raw-16>:<name-fdbstuffed>` → name pointer (for ListAccessPoints range scan via `access_point:by_bucket:<bucket-uuid>:` prefix).

**CreateAccessPoint**: single pessimistic txn writes all three index keys atomically. Returns meta.ErrAccessPointAlreadyExists (sentinel verified to exist at internal/meta/store.go:66) on duplicate name.
**GetAccessPoint(name)**: single snapshot Get on by_name key, decode JSON. Returns meta.ErrAccessPointNotFound (verified at store.go:67) on absent key. NO pessimistic txn — read-only.

**GetAccessPointByAlias(alias)**: snapshot Get on by_alias → derived name → snapshot Get on by_name → decode. NO pessimistic txn. **Race semantics**: if the second Get returns ErrAccessPointNotFound (concurrent Delete after first read), function MUST return ErrAccessPointNotFound (caller sees linearizable 'deleted' state). Document race resolution inline.
**DeleteAccessPoint(name)**: read by_name to derive alias + bucket → delete all three index keys atomically via pessimistic txn.

**ListAccessPoints(bucketID)**: range scan `access_point:by_bucket:<bucket-uuid>:` prefix → derive name list → snapshot Get each by_name in follow-up loop. Order matches Cassandra impl (likely lex by name).

**Cassandra parity** check against internal/meta/cassandra/store.go CreateAccessPoint/etc — same return shape, same sentinels. meta.AccessPoint struct verified to carry BucketID uuid.UUID field at store.go:911 — by_bucket index key has the data.
**Contract test additions** in contract.go: build on existing CreateAccessPoint cases (lines 1774, 1777) — add cases for GetByAlias, DeleteAccessPoint, ListAccessPoints with multi-AP seed.
`go vet ./...` passes; `go test -race ./internal/meta/...` passes across all three backends.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: SSE rewrap progress + raw manifest + replication status
userStories.description: As an operator running strata admin rewrap on a TiKV-backed deployment, I want rewrap progress checkpointing to work; as an operator running replication, I want per-object replication status writable.
acceptanceCriteria: 6 TiKV impls replacing stubs at lines 164-185: UpdateObjectSSEWrap, SetRewrapProgress, GetRewrapProgress, GetObjectManifestRaw, UpdateObjectManifestRaw, SetObjectReplicationStatus.

**UpdateObjectSSEWrap(bucket, key, version, wrapped, keyID)**: writes to existing `objects` table row. meta.Object struct ALREADY carries SSEKey []byte + SSEKeyID string fields (internal/meta/store.go:579-580) — TiKV objects payload already serializes them. Impl: pessimistic txn → Read existing object via established TiKV object CRUD path → set o.SSEKey = wrapped + o.SSEKeyID = keyID → write back. Match `versionID == ''` path (resolves to latest version) per Cassandra UpdateObjectSSEWrap lines 4015-4021. NOT a schema-additive change.
**SetRewrapProgress(progress)**: key `sse_rewrap_progress:<bucket-uuid-raw-16>` → *meta.RewrapProgress JSON blob. Pessimistic txn.
**GetRewrapProgress(bucket)**: snapshot Get; returns (nil, nil) on absent key. Verify Cassandra null-row semantics.

**GetObjectManifestRaw(bucket, key, version)**: reads manifest blob from existing `objects` row WITHOUT decoding. Snapshot Get + extract `manifest` field as raw bytes. Returns meta.ErrObjectNotFound on absent (matches Cassandra).
**UpdateObjectManifestRaw(bucket, key, version, raw)**: writes raw bytes to manifest field of existing `objects` row. Pessimistic txn.

**SetObjectReplicationStatus(bucket, key, version, status)**: writes `replication_status` field to existing `objects` row. Pessimistic txn. status ∈ {PENDING, COMPLETE, FAILED, REPLICA} (verify exact enum in Cassandra impl).
**Cassandra parity** check against 6 corresponding Cassandra impls — same return shape, same sentinels, same null-handling.

**Contract test additions** in contract.go: build on existing SetRewrapProgress (line 651) + GetObjectManifestRaw (lines 694/721/737/746) cases — add cases for missing UpdateObjectSSEWrap / UpdateObjectManifestRaw / SetObjectReplicationStatus.

**manifest_rewriter worker** (`strata server --workers=manifest-rewriter`) MUST keep working against TiKV — JSON→proto bulk conversion uses GetObjectManifestRaw + UpdateObjectManifestRaw. Strict smoke validation in US-006.
`go vet ./...` passes; `go test -race ./internal/meta/...` passes across all three backends.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Smoke validation + perf budget + ROADMAP close-flip + PRD removal
userStories.description: As a future-maintainer, I want one explicit verification pass that proves the 22 implementations landed + TiKV-default lab smoke passes the previously-failing legs + perf budget captured + ROADMAP entry flipped + PRD markdown removed.
acceptanceCriteria: **Pre-cycle baseline capture**: record current `make smoke` output against TiKV-default lab (expected: fails at TAGGING leg) BEFORE any US-001 impl lands. Stash in progress.txt as before-state.
Run `make smoke` against TiKV-default lab → green (TAGGING + retention legs pass).
Run `make smoke-signed` → green.
Run `make smoke-tikv-default-lab` → all 4 scenarios pass.
Run full `go test -race ./...` (default tag) → green; capture duration.
Run `make test-integration` (Cassandra testcontainers) → green (parity check — no regression).

**STRICT CI gate**: `go test ./internal/meta/tikv/... -tags integration` against testcontainers PD+TiKV MUST pass in CI before merge. If local box can't run testcontainers (lima docker context not aligned with host.docker.internal gateway alias per CLAUDE.md gotcha), document local SKIP in progress.txt AND verify CI workflow .github/workflows/ci-tikv.yml runs the suite via STRATA_TIKV_TEST_PD_ENDPOINTS against compose-managed PD. Merge to main BLOCKED until CI green — no exceptions.

Run scripts/s3-tests/run.sh against TiKV-default lab → record pass-rate. Should be ≥ pre-cycle baseline (Cassandra-default run). Update scripts/s3-tests/README.md baseline section per CLAUDE.md `## Commits and PRs` rule.
Run `make vet` + `make docs-build` → green.

**Manifest-rewriter strict smoke** (validates US-005's GetObjectManifestRaw + UpdateObjectManifestRaw against TiKV): (a) seed 10 objects with STRATA_MANIFEST_FORMAT=json against TiKV-default lab; (b) restart lab with STRATA_MANIFEST_FORMAT=proto + STRATA_WORKERS=manifest-rewriter env; (c) wait for one rewriter tick (set STRATA_MANIFEST_REWRITER_INTERVAL=10s for smoke); (d) read all 10 manifests via TiKV and assert proto-shaped (first byte not `{`). Capture seeded count + rewritten count in progress.txt.

**Perf budget**: bench TiKV p99 latency vs Cassandra baseline. Run tag-heavy workload (50k SetObjectTags + 50k GetObjectTags concurrent) against TiKV-default and Cassandra-default labs. Capture ratio in progress.txt: `TiKV/Cassandra p99 ratio = X.Yx`. If ratio > 2× for any method, ROADMAP gets new P3 entry `TiKV meta backend perf — Y method 2.Xx slower than Cassandra` parked under `## Scalability & performance`.

**ROADMAP close-flip × 1** on line 415: P2 entry `TiKV meta backend stubs 19 meta.Store methods` — the entry says 19 but actual count is **22**; close-flip text MUST correct the count. Summary references each of US-001..US-005 + contract test additions + smoke leg red→green + perf budget ratio.

**Fix the ROADMAP formatting bug at line 415** in same commit: P2 entry currently merged into prior P1 `Fixed` RADOS-probe block (visually one bullet, reads as two concatenated paragraphs). Split into proper standalone bullet during close-flip.
Close-flip carries `(commit pending)` placeholder per established convention; SHA backfill on main as fast-follow.
tasks/prd-tikv-stubs.md REMOVED via `git rm`.
scripts/ralph/progress.txt carries one US-006 block summarising before/after smoke + contract coverage delta + s3-tests pass-rate + perf budget ratio.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
